author: @encan99
text:
Hello. Thank you for the great video. My question is about the wire. How hard is it and does it hold its shape when bent. Does it have a code like 304 Steel? 

Also if there is a bend on the wire before winding does make inconsistant gaps in the finished winding?

author: @kdwaynec
text: Nice! I've wound hundreds of rods, worked for Buschman in Ohio for a while. A little more automated than this, but the smallest wire I wound was .003"! A real nightmare!

author: @DonBryantPhotography
text: So easy .... Yeah if you have a shop. Good work!

author: @mauriziocelentano7658
text: May I use a threaded rod for doing the same job?

author: @robertwjenkins6916
text: That's awesome to know...I am wondering if you have ever tried modifying an inkjet printer to use with your conductive inks? Or is that not a viable idea? Also..I do a bit of recycling of many things...would the stainless steel rollers in an office copier work? I've recycled many of them

author: @dolanbaker
text: The audio quality in the workshop is the best yet, well done.

author: @RWBHere
text: You can also use a variable speed electric drill, an electric screwdriver or a hand drill plus assistant for the job. Thanks Robert.

author: @Buzzhumma
text: Crazy I know but I had really good results with stainless mig welding wire on various sized  stainless threaded rod .

author: @anthonydonnelly4069
text: You never cease to surprise me.  Interesting.  Thanks

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome - I think lol - cheers mate

author: @Barskor1
text: The spiral pushes the ink off to a side a minor problem but a perfect one would be if you could just lath the cuts into the rod for depth and width but that would be far more expensive in skill time and tooling to do. Perhaps electrolysis cutting might work.

author: @Barskor1
text: That looks like a minimal skill job able to do 10 in an hour easily to make the drawbar so employing one person to do it is likely going to be viable.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it certainly didn't take me long

replies.author: @Barskor1
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Yes indeed the standard pricing and efforts required show a good opportunity if demand is steady.

author: @maciekm7953
text: Thank you for sharing👍 this is awesome

author: @stemcampprojects7951
text: Don't roll the bar - draw the bar.  Does this apply to threaded rods as well?  Do you get a better consistency with drawing versus rolling?  THANKS!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes it does and yes you do - rolling picks the ink up

author: @viliamholub509
text:
I found a steel rod in used cartridges for laser printers. I also found there a piece of thin metal that can be used as doctor’s blade 😀

You can use battery-powered screwdriver if you don’t own a lathe.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's a good idea mate - thanks for the share

author: @lebenshaus-werkstatt
text: Brillant!

author: @JayMayKnow
text:
When calculating the resulting thickness of the layer by  Wire Radius / %solids
It doesn't exclude the volume taken up by the wire. 
So the max height is indeed half the diameter of the wire times the % solids, 
But then the layer levels out from those peaks between the wire, to an average. 

Or am I missing something?     Great Video!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nope - you are spot on mate - you could always use a micrometer too!

replies.author: @gcewing
replies.text: Theoretically the thickness of liquid left behind should be 1-pi/4 times the wire radius. But you should probably measure if you care about the exact thickness, since the real world is nororiously bad at conforming to theories.

author: @MisterRorschach90
text: So simple but interesting. It’s crazy how much they sell unique premade tools for.

author: @richardteychenne3950
text: Simple, repeatable and elegant, not only that you can make it at home, superb, thanks Rob

author: @saber1epee0
text:
Learned a bit of this in University, doing RnD on gasket coatings. 

It was pretty great once I had practice with the bar, though like a drawdown knife it needs a bit of patience/practice with anything sticky

author: @William_Hada
text: Excellent video Rob! Relatively easy to make at quite a savings. I didn't know that you are supposed draw it accross and not roll it!  I searched for these metering rods on eBay a while ago and couldn't believe the money they wanted for used ones. Thanks for sharing!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no worries - glad you liked it mate
